moduleDescription:
Shared table support for Nim. Use plain old non GC'ed keys and values or you'll be in trouble. Uses a single lock to protect the table, lockfree implementations welcome but if lock contention is so high that you need a lockfree hash table, you're doing it wrong.

Unstable API.

entries.name: withValue
entries.code: template withValue[A, B](t: var SharedTable[A, B]; key: A; value, body: untyped)
entries.description: Retrieves the value at t[key]. value can be modified in the scope of the withValue call.

entries.name: withValue
entries.code:
template withValue[A, B](t: var SharedTable[A, B]; key: A;
                         value, body1, body2: untyped)
entries.description: Retrieves the value at t[key]. value can be modified in the scope of the withValue call.

entries.name: mget
entries.code: proc mget[A, B](t: var SharedTable[A, B]; key: A): var B
entries.description: Retrieves the value at t[key]. The value can be modified. If key is not in t, the KeyError exception is raised.

entries.name: mgetOrPut
entries.code: proc mgetOrPut[A, B](t: var SharedTable[A, B]; key: A; val: B): var B
entries.description: Retrieves value at t[key] or puts val if not present, either way returning a value which can be modified. Note: This is inherently unsafe in the context of multi-threading since it returns a pointer to B.

entries.name: hasKeyOrPut
entries.code: proc hasKeyOrPut[A, B](t: var SharedTable[A, B]; key: A; val: B): bool
entries.description: Returns true if key is in the table, otherwise inserts value.

entries.name: withKey
entries.code:
proc withKey[A, B](t: var SharedTable[A, B]; key: A;
                   mapper: proc (key: A; val: var B; pairExists: var bool))

arguments.name: mapper
arguments.type: proc (key: A; val: var B; pairExists: var bool)

entries.description:
Computes a new mapping for the key with the specified mapper procedure.

The mapper takes 3 arguments:

  1. key - the current key, if it exists, or the key passed to withKey otherwise;
  2. val - the current value, if the key exists, or default value of the type otherwise;
  3. pairExists - true if the key exists, false otherwise.

The mapper can can modify val and pairExists values to change the mapping of the key or delete it from the table. When adding a value, make sure to set pairExists to true along with modifying the val.

The operation is performed atomically and other operations on the table will be blocked while the mapper is invoked, so it should be short and simple.

Example usage:

# If value exists, decrement it.
# If it becomes zero or less, delete the key
t.withKey(1'i64) do (k: int64, v: var int, pairExists: var bool):
  if pairExists:
    dec v
    if v <= 0:
      pairExists = false

entries.code: proc `[]=`[A, B](t: var SharedTable[A, B]; key: A; val: B)
entries.description: Puts a (key, value)-pair into t.

entries.name: add
entries.code: proc add[A, B](t: var SharedTable[A, B]; key: A; val: B)
entries.description: Puts a new (key, value)-pair into t even if t[key] already exists. This can introduce duplicate keys into the table!

entries.name: del
entries.code: proc del[A, B](t: var SharedTable[A, B]; key: A)
entries.description: Deletes key from hash table t.

entries.name: len
entries.code: proc len[A, B](t: var SharedTable[A, B]): int
entries.description: Number of elements in t.

entries.name: init
entries.code: proc init[A, B](t: var SharedTable[A, B]; initialSize = 32)
entries.description:
Creates a new hash table that is empty.

This proc must be called before any other usage of t.

entries.name: deinitSharedTable
entries.code: proc deinitSharedTable[A, B](t: var SharedTable[A, B])
